title: Volice worm
content:
{{Top|ca|leg|notoc=1}}

The '''volice worm''',<ref name="FDCR" /> also known simply as the '''volice''', was a type of unusual<ref name="CSWE volice">{{CSWECite|III|310|volice}}</ref> massive [[Worm/Legends|worm]] that at one point lived on<ref name="FDCR" /> the [[Volcano/Legends|volcanically]] active, [[Jungle/Legends|jungle]]-covered<ref name="TABS">''[[The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook]]''</ref> [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Lwhekk/Legends|Lwhekk]]. It ingested [[Stone/Legends|rocks]] containing [[Crystal/Legends|crystalline]] substances that could be used as [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|focusing elements]] in the [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]] used by the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] tradition of [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitives]].<ref name="FDCR" /> Consequently, once every eleven [[Local year|years]] the volice worm excreted<ref name="KOTOR">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''</ref> a unique<ref name="CSWE volice" /> crystallized and translucent<ref name="KOTOR" /> substance known as [[sapith]].<ref name="CSWE volice" />

At some point long before<ref name="KOTOR" /> [[3956 BBY]]<ref name="3956 BBY">''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' dates the events of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' to [[3956 BBY]].</ref> and many [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]],<ref name="CSWE volice" /> the volice worm went extinct,<ref name="KOTOR" /> with certain specimens eventually becoming fossilized. Over [[Time/Legends|time]], individuals would excavate such volice worm remains in order to obtain the increasingly rare sapith crystals,<ref name="FDCR" /> which were used by ancient [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]]<ref name="CSWE sapith">{{CSWECite|III|127|sapith}}</ref> as lightsaber focusing crystals<ref name="CSWE volice" /> and were available in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]<ref name="KOTOR" /> by 3956 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]].<ref name="3956 BBY" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:KotOR_Cover.png|thumb|right|250px|The volice worm was introduced in ''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic''.]]
The volice worm was first mentioned in the [[2003]] [[BioWare]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''. The game,<ref name="KOTOR" /> as well as its [[2004]] [[Obsidian Entertainment]] sequel, ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''<ref name="KOTOR2">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''</ref> and its accompanying [[Prima Games]] [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords: Prima Official Game Guide|game guide]], capitalized the creature's name as "Volice worm."<ref name="KOTOR2Prima">[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords'': Prima Official Game Guide]]</ref> However, later sources have instead used a lowercase form of the name, which this article assumes takes precedence.<ref name="FDCR" /><ref name="CSWE volice" /><ref name="CSWE sapith" /> In addition, [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' introduced a shortened version of the volice worm's name, and one entry in the book also erroneously claims that the worm excreted the sapith crystals once every ten years<ref name="CSWE volice" /> instead of once every eleven years.<ref name="KOTOR" /><ref name="CSWE sapith" />
